summary: The manuscript presents a benchmark suite, but the comparative claims lack the statistical confidence intervals required to validate them. In structural science, a pattern is not interpreted until the error margins are quantified; the same rigor must apply here.
body: The benchmark claims are presented with high confidence, yet the statistical treatment of the comparative results is insufficient. A diffraction pattern is not accepted as proof of structure until the hydration and error parameters are defined; similarly, performance metrics must include confidence intervals and significance testing. The current tables list means without standard deviations. Strengthen the statistical analysis section to demonstrate that the observed differences are not artifacts of sampling variance.
